req.title: System-Admin views payroll-run
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a payroll-run through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Tests for Payroll Run View functionality.
Requirement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a payroll-run
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record
must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import date, datetime
from decimal import Decimal


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():
    """Authenticated system-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 1
    user.username = "admin_user"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.roles = ["system-admin"]
    user.has_role = lambda role: role in user.roles
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_user():
    """Authenticated user without system-admin role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 2
    user.username = "regular_user"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.roles = ["employee"]
    user.has_role = lambda role: role in user.roles
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Unauthenticated / anonymous user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.roles = []
    user.has_role = lambda role: False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_payroll_run():
    """A fully populated payroll-run record."""
    run = MagicMock()
    run.id = 101
    run.run_reference = "PR-2024-001"
    run.pay_period_start = date(2024, 1, 1)
    run.pay_period_end = date(2024, 1, 31)
    run.run_date = date(2024, 2, 1)
    run.status = "COMPLETED"
    run.total_gross = Decimal("250000.00")
    run.total_net = Decimal("187500.00")
    run.total_deductions = Decimal("62500.00")
    run.employee_count = 50
    run.created_by = "admin_user"
    run.created_at = datetime(2024, 2, 1, 9, 0, 0)
    return run


@pytest.fixture
def payroll_run_missing_mandatory_fields():
    """A payroll-run record missing mandatory fields."""
    run = MagicMock()
    run.id = 102
    run.run_reference = None          # mandatory – missing
    run.pay_period_start = None       # mandatory – missing
    run.pay_period_end = date(2024, 1, 31)
    run.run_date = None               # mandatory – missing
    run.status = None                 # mandatory – missing
    run.total_gross = None
    run.total_net = None
    run.total_deductions = None
    run.employee_count = None
    run.created_by = None
    run.created_at = None
    return run


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Mock audit-logging service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def payroll_service(valid_payroll_run):
    """Mock payroll service that returns a valid run by default."""
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.get_payroll_run.return_value = valid_payroll_run
    return svc


@pytest.fixture
def payroll_view(payroll_service, audit_service):
    """
    Construct the PayrollRunView under test.
    Replace with your actual import, e.g.:
        from myapp.views.payroll import PayrollRunView
    """
    from myapp.views.payroll import PayrollRunView   # adjust import path

    view = PayrollRunView(
        payroll_service=payroll_service,
        audit_service=audit_service,
    )
    return view


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Authentication & authorisation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAuthenticationAndAuthorisation:

    def test_system_admin_can_access_view(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user):
        """Authenticated system-admin receives HTTP 200 when viewing a payroll run."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_unauthenticated_user_is_redirected(self, payroll_view, unauthenticated_user):
        """Unauthenticated request is redirected to login (HTTP 302)."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=unauthenticated_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert response.status_code in (302, 401)

    def test_non_admin_user_is_forbidden(self, payroll_view, non_admin_user):
        """User without system-admin role receives HTTP 403."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=non_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_non_admin_user_cannot_view_payroll_data(self, payroll_view, non_admin_user,
                                                      payroll_service):
        """Payroll data is never retrieved for a non-admin user."""
        payroll_view.get(user=non_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.assert_not_called()

    def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_payroll_data(self, payroll_view,
                                                            unauthenticated_user,
                                                            payroll_service):
        """Payroll data is never retrieved for an unauthenticated user."""
        payroll_view.get(user=unauthenticated_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.assert_not_called()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Successful view – form rendering
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestSuccessfulPayrollRunView:

    def test_response_contains_run_reference(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                              valid_payroll_run):
        """Run reference is present in the rendered form."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert valid_payroll_run.run_reference in str(response.content)

    def test_response_contains_pay_period_start(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                                 valid_payroll_run):
        """Pay period start date is present in the rendered form."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert str(valid_payroll_run.pay_period_start) in str(response.content)

    def test_response_contains_pay_period_end(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                               valid_payroll_run):
        """Pay period end date is present in the rendered form."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert str(valid_payroll_run.pay_period_end) in str(response.content)

    def test_response_contains_run_date(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                        valid_payroll_run):
        """Run date is present in the rendered form."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert str(valid_payroll_run.run_date) in str(response.content)

    def test_response_contains_status(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                      valid_payroll_run):
        """Payroll run status is present in the rendered form."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert valid_payroll_run.status in str(response.content)

    def test_response_contains_total_gross(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                           valid_payroll_run):
        """Total gross amount is present in the rendered form."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert str(valid_payroll_run.total_gross) in str(response.content)

    def test_response_contains_total_net(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                         valid_payroll_run):
        """Total net amount is present in the rendered form."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert str(valid_payroll_run.total_net) in str(response.content)

    def test_response_contains_total_deductions(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                                 valid_payroll_run):
        """Total deductions are present in the rendered form."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert str(valid_payroll_run.total_deductions) in str(response.content)

    def test_response_contains_employee_count(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                               valid_payroll_run):
        """Employee count is present in the rendered form."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert str(valid_payroll_run.employee_count) in str(response.content)

    def test_form_fields_are_read_only(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user):
        """All form fields are rendered as read-only (view-only form)."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert response.form_is_read_only is True

    def test_correct_payroll_run_is_fetched(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                             payroll_service):
        """The service is called with the correct payroll-run ID."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.assert_called_once_with(101)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Mandatory field validation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:

    MANDATORY_FIELDS = [
        "run_reference",
        "pay_period_start",
        "pay_period_end",
        "run_date",
        "status",
    ]

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_raises_validation_error(
        self, payroll_view, system_admin_user, payroll_service,
        valid_payroll_run, missing_field
    ):
        """Each mandatory field, when absent, causes a validation error."""
        setattr(valid_payroll_run, missing_field, None)
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.return_value = valid_payroll_run

        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)

        assert response.status_code in (400, 422)
        assert missing_field in str(response.validation_errors)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_error_message_is_user_friendly(
        self, payroll_view, system_admin_user, payroll_service,
        valid_payroll_run, missing_field
    ):
        """Validation error messages are descriptive and user-facing."""
        setattr(valid_payroll_run, missing_field, None)
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.return_value = valid_payroll_run

        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)

        error_text = str(response.validation_errors).lower()
        assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ("required", "missing", "must not be blank"))

    def test_record_with_all_mandatory_fields_passes_validation(
        self, payroll_view, system_admin_user, valid_payroll_run
    ):
        """A fully populated record passes all mandatory-field checks."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert response.status_code == 200
        assert not response.validation_errors

    def test_validation_error_does_not_write_audit_record(
        self, payroll_view, system_admin_user, payroll_service,
        valid_payroll_run, audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record is written when mandatory field validation fails."""
        valid_payroll_run.run_reference = None
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.return_value = valid_payroll_run

        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)

        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_all_mandatory_fields_missing_returns_multiple_errors(
        self, payroll_view, system_admin_user, payroll_service,
        payroll_run_missing_mandatory_fields
    ):
        """When several mandatory fields are missing, all errors are reported at once."""
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.return_value = payroll_run_missing_mandatory_fields

        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=102)

        assert len(response.validation_errors) >= 4  # at least 4 mandatory fields absent


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Audit record
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAuditRecord:

    def test_audit_record_written_on_successful_view(self, payroll_view,
                                                      system_admin_user,
                                                      audit_service):
        """An audit record is written after a successful payroll-run view."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_service.write.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_user_id(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                            audit_service):
        """Audit record captures the ID of the acting user."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_call_kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args.kwargs
        assert audit_call_kwargs.get("user_id") == system_admin_user.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_username(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                             audit_service):
        """Audit record captures the username of the acting user."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_call_kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args.kwargs
        assert audit_call_kwargs.get("username") == system_admin_user.username

    def test_audit_record_contains_payroll_run_id(self, payroll_view,
                                                    system_admin_user,
                                                    audit_service):
        """Audit record references the payroll-run ID that was viewed."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_call_kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args.kwargs
        assert audit_call_kwargs.get("resource_id") == 101

    def test_audit_record_action_is_view(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                          audit_service):
        """Audit record action is labelled as VIEW (read-only operation)."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_call_kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args.kwargs
        assert audit_call_kwargs.get("action") == "VIEW"

    def test_audit_record_resource_type_is_payroll_run(self, payroll_view,
                                                         system_admin_user,
                                                         audit_service):
        """Audit record resource type identifies a payroll run."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_call_kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args.kwargs
        assert audit_call_kwargs.get("resource_type") == "PAYROLL_RUN"

    def test_audit_record_has_timestamp(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                         audit_service):
        """Audit record includes a timestamp of the view event."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_call_kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args.kwargs
        assert audit_call_kwargs.get("timestamp") is not None

    def test_audit_record_timestamp_is_recent(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                               audit_service):
        """Audit record timestamp is within 5 seconds of 'now'."""
        before = datetime.utcnow()
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        after = datetime.utcnow()

        audit_call_kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args.kwargs
        recorded_at = audit_call_kwargs.get("timestamp")

        assert before <= recorded_at <= after

    def test_audit_record_not_written_for_non_admin(self, payroll_view,
                                                      non_admin_user,
                                                      audit_service):
        """No audit record is written when access is denied (non-admin)."""
        payroll_view.get(user=non_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_not_written_for_unauthenticated(self, payroll_view,
                                                            unauthenticated_user,
                                                            audit_service):
        """No audit record is written for an unauthenticated request."""
        payroll_view.get(user=unauthenticated_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_written_exactly_once_per_view(self, payroll_view,
                                                          system_admin_user,
                                                          audit_service):
        """Exactly one audit record is written per successful view request."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert audit_service.write.call_count == 1

    def test_audit_record_written_for_each_separate_view(self, payroll_view,
                                                           system_admin_user,
                                                           audit_service):
        """Each distinct view request produces its own audit record."""
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert audit_service.write.call_count == 2


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Edge-case & error-handling
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestEdgeCasesAndErrorHandling:

    def test_non_existent_payroll_run_returns_404(self, payroll_view,
                                                    system_admin_user,
                                                    payroll_service):
        """Requesting a payroll run that does not exist returns HTTP 404."""
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.return_value = None
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=9999)
        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_non_existent_payroll_run_does_not_write_audit(self, payroll_view,
                                                             system_admin_user,
                                                             payroll_service,
                                                             audit_service):
        """No audit record is written when the requested run does not exist."""
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.return_value = None
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=9999)
        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_invalid_payroll_run_id_type_returns_400(self, payroll_view,
                                                       system_admin_user):
        """A non-numeric payroll-run ID returns HTTP 400."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id="abc")
        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_payroll_service_exception_returns_500(self, payroll_view,
                                                    system_admin_user,
                                                    payroll_service):
        """An unexpected service error is surfaced as HTTP 500."""
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.side_effect = RuntimeError("DB connection lost")
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert response.status_code == 500

    def test_payroll_service_exception_does_not_write_audit(self, payroll_view,
                                                              system_admin_user,
                                                              payroll_service,
                                                              audit_service):
        """No audit record is written when the service raises an exception."""
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.side_effect = RuntimeError("DB connection lost")
        payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_service_failure_does_not_suppress_response(self, payroll_view,
                                                                system_admin_user,
                                                                audit_service):
        """
        If the audit service itself raises an exception the view must still
        propagate the error (it must not silently swallow it).
        """
        audit_service.write.side_effect = IOError("Audit store unavailable")
        with pytest.raises(IOError):
            payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)

    def test_zero_employee_count_is_valid(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user,
                                           payroll_service, valid_payroll_run):
        """A payroll run with zero employees is still a valid record to display."""
        valid_payroll_run.employee_count = 0
        payroll_service.get_payroll_run.return_value = valid_payroll_run
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_large_payroll_run_id_is_handled(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user):
        """Very large integer IDs are accepted without error."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=10**18)
        # Either 200 (found) or 404 (not found) – not a 400/500
        assert response.status_code in (200, 404)
```

---

## Notes

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Fixtures** | Shared via `conftest`-style fixtures; `MagicMock` keeps the tests free from real infrastructure. |
| **Import path** | `from myapp.views.payroll import PayrollRunView` — replace with your actual module path. |
| **`response` contract** | The tests assume the view returns an object with `.status_code`, `.content`, `.validation_errors`, and `.form_is_read_only`. Map these to your framework's response object (Django `HttpResponse`, Flask `Response`, etc.). |
| **`audit_service.write` signature** | All audit assertions use keyword arguments (`user_id`, `username`, `action`, `resource_type`, `resource_id`, `timestamp`). Align with your actual audit API. |
| **Parametrize** | Mandatory-field tests are parametrised so adding a new mandatory field only requires updating the `MANDATORY_FIELDS` list. |
| **Negative audit tests** | Explicitly verify the audit service is **not** called on auth failures, validation failures, and service errors — preventing false positives. |

Tests for Payroll Run View functionality.
Requirement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a payroll-run
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record
must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import date, datetime
from decimal import Decimal


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():

    user.username = "admin_user"

    user.has_role = lambda role: role in user.roles

    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_payroll_run():
    """A fully populated payroll-run record."""
    run = MagicMock()
    run.id = 101
    run.run_reference = "PR-2024-001"
    run.pay_period_start = date(2024, 1, 1)
    run.pay_period_end = date(2024, 1, 31)
    run.run_date = date(2024, 2, 1)
    run.status = "COMPLETED"

    run.total_deductions = Decimal("62500.00")
    run.employee_count = 50
    run.created_by = "admin_user"
    run.created_at = datetime(2024, 2, 1, 9, 0, 0)
    return run


@pytest.fixture
def payroll_run_missing_mandatory_fields():
    """A payroll-run record missing mandatory fields."""
    run = MagicMock()
    run.id = 102
    run.run_reference = None          # mandatory – missing
    run.pay_period_start = None       # mandatory – missing
    run.pay_period_end = date(2024, 1, 31)
    run.run_date = None               # mandatory – missing
    run.status = None                 # mandatory – missing
    run.total_gross = None
    run.total_net = None
    run.total_deductions = None
    run.employee_count = None
    run.created_by = None
    run.created_at = None

@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Mock audit-logging service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def payroll_service(valid_payroll_run):
    """Mock payroll service that returns a valid run by default."""
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.get_payroll_run.return_value = valid_payroll_run
    return svc


@pytest.fixture
def payroll_view(payroll_service, audit_service):
    """

    view = PayrollRunView(
        payroll_service=payroll_service,
        audit_service=audit_service,

    return view

# 1. Authentication & authorisation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAuthenticationAndAuthorisation:

    def test_system_admin_can_access_view(self, payroll_view, system_admin_user):
        """Authenticated system-admin receives HTTP 200 when viewing a payroll run."""
        response = payroll_view.get(user=system_admin_user, payroll_run_id=101)
        assert response.status_code == 200
